description: sprintf/strcpy/strcat called with only hardcoded string literals — no taint source. Layer D suppression (src/ast.rs::is_c_buffer_call_literal_safe) correctly recognises strcpy/strcat with string-literal source as bounded-length: only the sprintf with bare `%s` in its format keeps firing.

expected.notes: sprintf format `"Hello, %s!"` has bare `%s` so Layer D refuses to suppress — the pattern rule fires correctly.
